Politics.headline: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica's new president
Politics.detail: Laura Fernandez of the right-wing party assumed the presidency of Costa Rica, with her party holding an absolute majority in the legislature.

Politics.headline: Farage marks 'historic' political shift as Starmer insists he won't step down
Politics.detail: Reform UK has gained hundreds of seats in England's local elections and has gained control of two councils, Newcastle-under-Lyme and Havering, marking a significant shift in the political landscape.

Politics.headline: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
Politics.detail: Tensions escalate as the US and Iran engage in clashes in Hormuz, raising concerns about the ongoing conflict and its implications.

Politics.headline: Tamil Nadu government formation: AMMK chief Dhinakaran refutes party's support to TVK
Politics.detail: AMMK chief Dhinakaran has refuted claims of his party's support for TVK in Tamil Nadu, accusing TVK of submitting a forged letter to the Governor.

Politics.headline: Minister Joly to discuss the power of shared prosperity at the 2026 Global Progress Action Summit
Politics.detail: The Honourable Mélanie Joly will engage in a discussion with Pete Buttigieg at the Global Progress Action Summit, focusing on economic growth and the significance of governance in an interconnected world.

Finance & Economy.headline: Wall Street stocks rise on US job market resilience
Finance & Economy.detail: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices both reached new records following strong US jobs data, while the Dow ended flat; however, US consumer confidence is at an all-time low due to concerns about high prices and the US-Israel war on Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump Economy Surges with Strong Job Growth
Finance & Economy.detail: The US economy added 115,000 jobs in April, exceeding economists' expectations, with manufacturing seeing significant gains and the federal workforce shrinking under President Trump.

Finance & Economy.headline: Geopolitical Tensions, Oil Shock Top Stability Concerns
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent report indicates that geopolitical risks and the oil shock from ongoing conflicts are major concerns for financial stability, potentially fueling inflation and slowing economic growth.

Finance & Economy.headline: Zambia begins strategic fuel depot rehabilitation amid Middle East conflicts
Finance & Economy.detail: Zambia is rehabilitating its strategic fuel storage depots to increase fuel reserves and protect the country from supply disruptions caused by Middle East conflicts; the government has also suspended excise duty and zero-rated VAT on petrol and diesel to stabilize prices.

Finance & Economy.headline: Fed report: Rising concerns about global conflict, gas prices
Finance & Economy.detail: The Federal Reserve's recent Financial Stability Report indicates growing concerns about global conflicts, rising oil prices, AI, and cybersecurity issues, with geopolitical risks being the primary worry for most respondents.

Technology.headline: Anthropic signs $1.8 billion AI cloud deal with Akamai, Bloomberg News reports
Technology.detail: AI startup Anthropic has signed a $1.8 billion computing deal with Akamai Technologies to meet surging demand for its AI software. Akamai shares rose significantly after disclosing a long-term cloud deal with a frontier model provider.

Technology.headline: Apple, Intel reach preliminary chip-making deal, WSJ reports
Technology.detail: Apple and Intel have reportedly reached a preliminary chip-making deal after intensive talks, with Intel's stock rising on the news. This deal would provide Intel with a steady demand stream from Apple, boosting its manufacturing business.

Technology.headline: Samsung, Meta want to make movement in the AI market with humanoid robots
Technology.detail: Samsung Electronics is expected to unveil a humanoid robot as early as the second half of this year, according to industry sources. Samsung is planning to introduce manufacturing-focused humanoids and dual-arm robots initially.

Technology.headline: DXC Technology (DXC) Q3 2026 Earnings Margin Recovery Challenges Bearish Profitability Narrative
Technology.detail: DXC Technology posted its Q3 2026 numbers with revenue of US$3.2 billion and basic EPS of US$0.62. The company has seen steady quarterly revenue and improving per share profitability.

Technology.headline: OpenAI's latest API models bring live translation and transcription to voice apps
Technology.detail: OpenAI's latest API models bring live translation and transcription to voice apps, enhancing voice technology capabilities. These models promise to improve the accuracy and integration of voice commands in various applications.

Science.headline: Scientists Turn Cancer's Own Bacteria Against It in Breakthrough Therapy
Science.detail: A newly developed therapy inspired by bacteria residing within tumors offers a different way to treat cancer.

Science.headline: Blue Origin's New Moon Lander Passes a Crucial Test for NASA Missions
Science.detail: Blue Origin's MK1 lander just survived a brutal dress rehearsal for the Moon, marking a bold step toward returning humans to the Moon.

Science.headline: Dinosaur dental fossils reveal bird-like parental care bonds
Science.detail: Fossils show that dinosaurs exhibited parental care similar to birds.

Science.headline: Northern Sri Lanka's oldest confirmed settlement reshapes what archaeologists thought about early island life
Science.detail: Archaeological findings in Northern Sri Lanka are changing perspectives on the early history of island life.

Science.headline: Scientists may have uncovered a surprising secret behind why life exists at all
Science.detail: A new study suggests that the Universe's fundamental constants may hold the key to why life exists.

Health.headline: Kenya issues alert over hantavirus outbreak
Health.detail: Kenyan authorities have issued a public health advisory following a hantavirus outbreak on the cruise ship MV Hondius, with intensified surveillance at all points of entry and no reported cases in Kenya. Health authorities are closely monitoring the situation and taking precautionary measures, including enhanced surveillance and preparedness systems.

Health.headline: Africa CDC calls for vigilance against hantavirus despite low risk to Africa
Health.detail: The Africa CDC has urged precautionary measures against hantavirus, while assessing the outbreak as posing only a minimal risk to the continent, following a cluster of infections on the cruise ship MV Hondius. The agency is working closely with Cabo Verde and South Africa, the two African countries currently linked to the virus outbreak, and is coordinating an international response.

Health.headline: Cabo Verde says Andes virus detected in 2 evacuees from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
Health.detail: Laboratory tests in Cabo Verde confirmed recent infection with Andes virus, a type of hantavirus, in two of the three patients evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship. Genomic sequencing is underway for a full characterization of the identified viral strain, and the country's National IHR Focal Point has notified its counterparts in concerned countries.

Health.headline: Tainan AI health checks upgraded: one X-ray helps track osteoporosis and cardiovascular risks
Health.detail: Tainan City has upgraded its Mobile Hospital program with AI-assisted image analysis, allowing chest X-rays to provide information on bone health and cardiovascular risks. The AI analysis assists physicians in interpreting results and providing follow-up advice, with a "Health Co-Care Cloud" platform for residents to view reports and access health advice.

Health.headline: What does Reform UK mean for healthcare?
Health.detail: An article discusses the potential impact of Reform UK on healthcare. This article may cover proposed changes, potential consequences, and analysis of their healthcare policies.

Sports.headline: Sources: Jets, Breece Hall agreed to a contract extension
Sports.detail: Hall has averaged more than 1,400 yards from scrimmage each of the past three seasons, securing his future with the New York Jets.

Sports.headline: Anunoby out for Game 3, Knicks turn to McBride
Sports.detail: OG Anunoby is out for Game 3, forcing the New York Knicks to adjust their lineup and rely on McBride.

Sports.headline: Liverpool Scales Back Plans For Ticket Price Increases After Fans Protests
Sports.detail: Following protests from fans, Liverpool has decided to scale back their plans for increasing ticket prices.

Sports.headline: NFL announces schedule release date for 2026 season
Sports.detail: The NFL has announced the date when the schedule for the 2026 season will be released.

Sports.headline: Sixers out to early lead over Knicks in Game 3; Paul George continues hot streak; New York fans draw boos after interrupting moment of silence
Sports.detail: The Sixers jumped to an early lead against the Knicks in Game 3, Paul George continued his hot streak, and New York fans were booed for interrupting a moment of silence.

Entertainment.headline: Olivia Rodrigo's 'Unraveled' Tour 2026: Ticket Prices, Presale Date & More
Entertainment.detail: Get all the information you need on tickets and dates for Olivia Rodrigo's 'Unraveled' Tour.

Entertainment.headline: Bad Bunny at the Met Gala 2026: See Photos of His Aged Look
Entertainment.detail: See photos of Bad Bunny's decades-aged appearance at the 2026 Met Gala.

Entertainment.headline: Dave Coulier Shares Heartbreaking Health Update After Major Weight Loss From Cancer Treatments
Entertainment.detail: Dave Coulier shares an emotional health update following significant weight loss due to cancer treatments.

Entertainment.headline: An Iranian museum holds a rare exhibit of American art, reflecting on war.
Entertainment.detail: An Iranian museum is hosting a unique exhibition of American art, offering reflections on the theme of war.

Entertainment.headline: Anthony Mason launches YouTube show
Entertainment.detail: CBS News' Anthony Mason is launching a new YouTube show called "Alchemy with Anthony Mason," a long-form interview program celebrating artists and the creative process.

Crime & Justice.headline: Four Defendants Convicted in Plot to Kill Haitian President Jovenel Moïse
Crime & Justice.detail: In a U.S. trial, four individuals were convicted for their involvement in the conspiracy surrounding the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse, highlighting Florida's role as a central hub in the planning. The assassination instigated an ongoing political crisis in Haiti.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man denies charge of threatening behaviour toward ex-Prince Andrew
Crime & Justice.detail: Alex Jenkinson, 39, pleaded not guilty to using threatening, abusive, or insulting language or behavior towards the former Prince Andrew near his home on King Charles III's Sandringham Estate. The incident occurred after reports that Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was threatened by a masked man while walking dogs.

Crime & Justice.headline: Egyptian jailed for life for attack on Jewish protest
Crime & Justice.detail: Mohamed Soliman, a 46-year-old Egyptian, was sentenced to life imprisonment for launching a Molotov cocktail attack on Jewish protesters in Colorado last year, which resulted in one death and multiple injuries. Soliman admitted to charges, including first-degree murder, for the attack that targeted a group supporting Israeli hostages held by Hamas.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Moves to Denaturalize 12 Individuals for Concealing Terrorist Support, War Crimes, Espionage, Sexual Abuse, and More
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department is seeking to revoke the citizenship of 12 individuals who are accused of concealing serious offenses, including providing terrorist support, committing war crimes, engaging in espionage, and perpetrating sexual abuse. This action underscores the department's commitment to ensuring that those who fraudulently obtain citizenship are held accountable.

Crime & Justice.headline: Indecent behaviour, indecent justice: How police misuse the law to arbitrarily detain citizens
Crime & Justice.detail: Police in Kathmandu are allegedly misusing the law related to "indecent behavior" to arbitrarily detain citizens, with records showing a high conviction rate in such cases. Legal experts and rights advocates argue that the police are abusing this law, leading to human rights violations and unnecessary suffering for vulnerable individuals.

Education.headline: Ministry pushes for university integration to boost workforce readiness
Education.detail: The Education Ministry in Fiji is strengthening efforts to better align universities with industry needs to close the gap between academic learning and workforce readiness, calling for deeper collaboration between higher education institutions, employers, and industry stakeholders.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines (May 9, 2026): National, International, Economy & Business Sports, Education and Science & Technology News Updates
Education.detail: Today's headlines highlight a transformative era in global trade, significant shifts in Indian regional politics, and pioneering milestones in space and medical science, including developments on the India-EU Free Trade Agreement and triumphs in the IPL and Archery World Cup.

Education.headline: New Recruitment Rules Enhance Child Safety in Education
Education.detail: New rules in NSW, Australia, require early learning services to undertake strict screening checks and verify suitability to work with children, including confirming that prospective and existing staff are not subject to suspension or prohibition notices.

Education.headline: Several Canadian universities face security breach, student data leaked
Education.detail: The U of T, UBC and U of A are amongst the major Canadian post-secondary institutions dealing with fallout from a cybersecurity incident involving a popular platform called Canvas.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights Opens Disability Discrimination Investigation into Texas School District
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education's Office for Civil Rights opened an investigation into the Houston Independent School District in Houston.

Lifestyle.headline: Mandira Bedi Shares Her Go-To "Shoulder Moves" For Toned, Strong Arms
Lifestyle.detail: At 54, Mandira Bedi shares her favorite shoulder exercises for achieving toned and strong arms. She emphasizes the importance of incorporating these moves into a regular fitness routine.

Lifestyle.headline: I didn't think I could get addicted to weed. I was wrong – and I'm not alone
Lifestyle.detail: The author reflects on their unexpected addiction to weed and highlights that they are not the only one struggling with this issue. This personal essay sheds light on the potential for cannabis addiction.

Lifestyle.headline: Millsboro pop-up event is for all the good causes
Lifestyle.detail: A non-profit pop-up event is scheduled for Saturday, May 9, from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m., inviting the public to connect with local outreach organizations. The event aims to promote community engagement and support for various causes.

Lifestyle.headline: Kris Jenner Reveals Why She Quit Ozempic: 'Made Me Sick And Nauseous'
Lifestyle.detail: Kris Jenner disclosed that she stopped using Ozempic because it caused her to feel sick and nauseous. She shared her experience with the drug and its adverse effects on her health.

Lifestyle.headline: Soft armour, pert nipples: how London design team made Kim Kardashian's Met Gala breastplate
Lifestyle.detail: A London-based design team created Kim Kardashian's Met Gala breastplate, showcasing innovative design and craftsmanship. The article delves into the creative process and inspiration behind the unique garment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Britain's fractured politics: a revolt against the status quo
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The UK political landscape is increasingly fragmented as evidenced by recent election results, signaling a rejection of traditional political approaches.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Resisting the rat invasion
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Increased rat sightings in Taipei have sparked concerns over urban sanitation and the government's ability to respond effectively, necessitating coordinated sanitation and waste management strategies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — May 9, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Hindu publishes letters to the editor, including one discussing Gopalkrishna Gandhi's advice to Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam leader C. Joseph Vijay, emphasizing careful consideration and caution in political governance.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A meditation on power and reluctance
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Cyrain Cabuenas reflects on the rare political talent of saying no, highlighting examples from history and the Church, and discussing a priest's decision to decline a bishop appointment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Corrections and Clarifications — May 9, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Hindu issues a correction stating that India is the world's second-largest coal producer, not third as previously mentioned in a report published on May 6, 2026.

World.headline: Trump announces three-day ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine war
World.detail: President Trump announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, with both countries confirming the agreement. The ceasefire also includes a prisoner swap.

World.headline: US fires on Iranian oil tanker as Trump pressures Tehran for deal to end war
World.detail: The U.S. military engaged with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz, firing on and disabling two Iranian oil tankers. This action comes amid heightened tensions and efforts to push Tehran towards a new agreement.

World.headline: Search resumes for 3 hikers, including 2 Singaporeans, after Mount Dukono eruption in Indonesia
World.detail: Rescuers are continuing their search for three hikers, including two Singaporeans, after the Mount Dukono volcano erupted in Indonesia. Hopes remain that the hikers will be found alive.

World.headline: CIA analysis suggests Iran could withstand blockade for 4 more months
World.detail: Despite recent clashes, a U.S. intelligence analysis indicates that Iran could endure a naval blockade for another four months. Efforts to end the war between the U.S. and Iran appeared to stall as the two sides traded fire in the Gulf on Friday.

World.headline: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica's new president
World.detail: Laura Fernandez has been sworn in as the new president of Costa Rica. The inauguration took place on May 8, 2026.

Zimbabwe.headline: Drug resistance risk as illicit medicine sales flourish in Harare
Zimbabwe.detail: An underground network of pharmacies and informal traders in Harare are illegally selling prescription medicines without doctors' scripts, raising concerns about drug resistance. This practice poses a significant threat to public health as it encourages the misuse and overuse of antibiotics and other critical medications.

Zimbabwe.headline: Modern facilities boost healthcare . . . President lauds partnerships. . . commits to improving conditions of service
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has reaffirmed the government's commitment to improving conditions for civil servants, including those in the health sector, emphasizing that disruptions affect patients, the economy, and the general public. He also lauded partnerships that boost healthcare.

Zimbabwe.headline: President mourns Mogae
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has expressed his deep sorrow following the death of former Botswana President Dr. Festus Mogae, describing him as a distinguished Pan-Africanist and principled leader. Mogae passed away at the age of 86.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe urges EU to lift remaining sanctions
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is calling on the European Union to lift the remaining sanctions. The government aims to rebuild relationships with Western countries and international lenders after years of isolation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Presidential programme upgrades hospitals, expands nurses' training
Zimbabwe.detail: The Presidential Hospitals Innovation Programme is being praised for modernizing health infrastructure and transforming service delivery at major hospitals, aligning with the Second Republic's vision. This program aims to improve healthcare facilities and expand training opportunities for nurses.
